_comment: REAL community compatibility reports ingested from EmuReady's public mobile API (https://www.emuready.com/api/mobile/trpc/listings.get) by tools/ingest/ingest.py. source=EMUREADY_SNAPSHOT → the recommender treats these as REAL (non-heuristic). Each report links back to its EmuReady listing via sourceRef (attribution). Data is community-contributed on EmuReady. dataAsOf: 2026-06-08. DO NOT hand-edit; regenerate with the ingest tool.

reports.notes: Performance is between 54-60fps even with high performance mode and fan at full speed.

The game works perfect outside of an initial stutter when you first load the game. I had consistant crashes at the Citadel Gatekeeper fight after trying every 3 times. And Gamenative WILL NOT do any cutscenes. Find for traveling because you can skip the cutscene but anywhere else the game just stalls and a reset is mandatory.

To use SenPatcher and its fixes:
Open container and install it first with the .exe (or manually) - the .exe will handily pick up the right place
Then make sure you set this variable in the "Environment" tab of the container settings
WINEDLLOVERRIDES
DINPUT8=n,b

NOTE: once this variable is set, the executable path must be ed8.exe - SenLauncher will no longer launch with it set. To change settings, either remove the variable, or open container and find the SenLauncher.exe in the A drive

First you will need to go into the game then Options then Video then Widescreen 16:9. Then Advanced, set Shader, Model/Texture Detail and Effect Detail to Medium. Helps reduce stuttering and makes it run 120FPS no problem. Perfectly playable and looks great. This also works for Portal Reloaded.

On the first boot, you must change a setting, otherwise you literally can't move UP in the menu and in-game you will constantly move up.

I wanted to play on game native. Finally got it with a few tweaks.

Runs at a constant 58 fps with dips to 38 fps at random locations of the map. This is not frequent tho.I personally capped the fps to 40 for a 100% stable and smooth experience. Absolutely zero problems if you play this way and I highly recommend it. Also played without phone cooler and the battery stayed well. Also I used proton 10.0-4 arm64 and Box64 version was 0.4.2 (these werent options in emuready, but theyre available in gamenative)
its just already so optimized and its a valve game from 2012 of course it runs perfectly

In order for it to run great I had to do a couple things, I turned resolution to 960x540, put scaling mode to pixel perfect, turning off V-Sync, putting shader quality to medium along with debris quantity. The game stays between 30-60fps though the stutter caused by that felt weird so i limited it to 30 fps

**{Important Notice}** I ran into a couple of problems while testing this game, but they seem to be quirks of the game rather than being issues with the emulator.
+ The Steam Cloud saves don't seem to be working so I had to create an account in the game to use their multi-platform saving system.
+ When you open the game the cursor is messed up and will constantly go to the bottom of the screen. I've found that pressing on the touchscreen and moving the mouse cursor around with fix it. 
+ The gam

Very infrequently, I had an odd glitch where, upon victory in a match, I could feel the vibration motor suddenly stay on, and controls would stop working, although emulation had not frozen. This occurred when I was playing Samus, if anyone wants to try and recreate the issue.

I restarted the game at 1.5x res with no VSync and it's doing really well!

I had to launch from inside the container on first run, exe is located in A:>_windows>win64. After that the game ran fine via shortcut. Played through the tutorial without issues at full speed. Cinematic videos are stuttery though.

Plays, and even plays around 30fps, but even with frameskip on, the engine can't keep the framerate and frame time in lock step, so you get slow downs where a 6 second cooldown can take as much as 12 seconds. The game remains responsive and playable during this slowdown, just events take longer. The further you get in the game the worse it is. The first map is almost completely playable.

Default settings runs the game but is a little slow and the intro logos and cutscenes are broken. The game will stay on a black screen until you hit Start or A, because the cutscenes aren't playing properly. 

The main game will still run. If you don't care about the cinematics, you can skip the fixes below.

Performance: 
- GameNative: Switch to VKD3D renderer
- Lower the in-game graphics settings:
. Anisotropic Filtering: 4 or lower
. Post Processing Quality: Medium or lower
. Ambient Occlusio
